name: orders
title: Orders
description: Sales and purchase orders (doc_type on one shared schema, mirroring app-invoices' doc_type split). Lines live in order_lines; subtotal/tax/total are stamped by the order_totals handler, never typed by hand. Reconciled against a private predecessor-system audit, not part of this repo (its Order+Lines note: 'SO/PO; convert_to_invoice').

fields.name: doc_type
fields.label: Document Type
fields.help: sale/purchase share this schema -- the real predecessor's SO/PO split, carried as one field the same way app-invoices folds invoice/credit_note/quote into doc_type.

fields.name: number
fields.label: Number
fields.help: Human order number, e.g. SO-0001 or PO-0001. v1 is typed by the owner, not auto-assigned -- a sequenced counter + closed create route is a later slice, same deferral app-invoices documents for its own number field.

fields.name: customer_id
fields.label: Customer (contact)
fields.help: Optional cross-reference only. The snapshot fields below are what every surface renders -- editing the contact later never rewrites an already-placed order. Same snapshot decision as app-invoices' customer_id.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.help: delivered and cancelled are terminal. Every other move requires the order owner. Matches the real order lifecycle (reconciled against a private predecessor-system audit, not part of this repo): draft->confirmed/cancelled, confirmed->processing/cancelled, processing->shipped/cancelled, shipped->delivered.

fields.name: subtotal_cents
fields.label: Subtotal (cents)
fields.help: Stamped by the order_totals handler: sum of every line's line_total_cents. Never user-entered -- not schema read_only for the same reason invoice_totals' fields aren't; see objects/system/order_totals.py's module docstring.

fields.name: tax_cents
fields.label: Tax (cents)
fields.help: Stamped by order_totals: sum of every line's line_tax_cents (line_total_cents * tax_rate_bps // 10000, floored).

fields.name: invoice_id
fields.label: Converted Invoice
fields.help: Provenance link recorded once this order has been converted to an invoice (real predecessor's convert_to_invoice). The conversion ACTION itself is deferred -- not built in this slice -- but the field is kept now so a future convert-to-invoice flow has somewhere to write without a backfill.
